prompt:
Our deploy wrapper mangles anything with a space in it. Today someone finally used a real deploy message:

    bash release.sh --message 'hotfix for cart totals' --target web1

and the request spool came out as argc=8 with the message broken across three separate fields, so the executor rejected the request as malformed. I ran the deployer directly with the exact same arguments:

    bash deploy.sh --message 'hotfix for cart totals' --target web1

and that spool is perfect — argc=4, message intact on one line. So deploy.sh itself is fine; it's the front door.

Two more oddities from this week that I'd bet are the same thing: a teammate passed --include 'notes/*.md' (meant literally — the deployer treats that field as a pattern spec) and the spool showed two real filenames from his checkout instead of the pattern he typed; and an intentionally blank --note '' just vanishes from the spool entirely, argc says 5 when it should say 6.

Plain single-word invocations like `bash release.sh --target web1` have worked for months, which is why nobody caught this earlier.

Fix release.sh so every field arrives at deploy.sh exactly as typed. `bash test_argfwd.sh` is the regression gate — leave the tests alone, and don't change the spool format; the executor side depends on it.
